Dr. Eng. Abolghasem Sadeghi-Niaraki

##########################################################

Dr. Eng. Abolghasem Sadeghi-Niaraki

##########################################################

Abolghasem Sadeghi-Niaraki (м•„ліјк°Җм…Ҳ) is an Associate Professor of Dept. Computer Science and Engineering, XR (eXtended Reality) Metaverse Research Center, Sejong University, the Republic of Korea since 2017. From 2009 to 2017, he was an Assistant Professor at Geo-Informatics Eng. Dept., INHA University. Dr. Abolghasem was recognized as one of the top 100 international distinguished researchers by the Australian Government and received the Australian Endeavour Fellowship Award, in 2012. Following this award, he joined the Department of Infrastructure Engineering at the University of Melbourne, Australia, from May to Oct. 2012. He received a B.Sc. degree in geomatics-civil Eng. and a M.Sc. degree in geospatial information system (GIS) Eng. from the Faculty of Geomatics Eng., K. N. Toosi University of Technology (KNTU), and the Ph.D. degree in Geo-Informatics Eng. from INHA University from South Korea in 2008.

He has a strong publication record and has published various peer-reviewed research articles in reputed international journals. In addition to having a variety of teaching experiences at several universities (INHA, Sejong and KNTU University), he has strong networks inside the Korean & Iranian private and governmental sectors. He has developed a strong background in leadership, sustainability, and collaborative research in science and technology using mixed research methodology between Iran and South Korea.

In Iran, he was a GIS/IT project manager at the Iranian Ministry of Roads and Urban Development, Tehran, from 2002 to 2005. He was selected as a national Distinguished Researcher of the Year, in 2003, and has been given the Distinguished Researcher Award by Iranian President (President Mohammad Khatami). Also, he is recognized as a top researcher at the Ministry of Roads and Transportation, having received the Best Researcher Award from the Minister of Roads, Mr. Khorram. Dr. Abolghasem also was a technology advisor of the Iranian Vice-Presidency for Science and Technology, Iranian Presidency. In Korea, he has consulting experience for Korean companies such as HANCOM Group (н•ңкёҖкіјм»ҙн“Ён„°), KSIC.net (гҲңн•ңкөӯкіөк°„м •ліҙнҶөмӢ ), and so on. He has also organized and directed various Hi-Tech based workshops and seminars in Iran by Korean experts. During his long time experiences working in Korea, he tried to do various activities which some of his most significant activities are as follows:

  • Performing and consulting for a ubiquitous GIS project at Gimpo Airport in Seoul. In this project, a new generation of GIS projects was developed to provide services to passengers from home to internal navigation in the GIS mobile environment, utilizing Context Awareness technology using Artificial intelligence in Seoul for this airport, collaborating with Korean teams.
  • Establishing and running a joint international postgraduate program in the field of Culture Technology in Iran between KAIST (Graduate School of Culture Technology) and one of the most prestigious Universities of Technology (KNTU).
  • Proposing and receiving a Culture Technology-based national project fund from the Presidency of Iran (Vice-Presidency for Science and Technology) to launch a Culture Technology Program supported by Graduate School of Culture Technology, KAIST.
  • Performing various research in the field of VR (Virtual Reality), MR (Mixed Reality) and GIS (Geographic Information System) via Large research project at MVR-RC (Mobile Virtual Reality Research Center) which approved and fully accepted by the MSIT (Ministry of Science and ICT), Korea, under the ITRC (Information Technology Research Center) support program supervised by the IITP (Institute for Information & communications Technology Planning & Evaluation).
  • Collaborating with ETRI to get a KoreaвҖҷs Knowledge Sharing Program (KSP) project via the Korean government. This project was a policy-oriented development cooperation program in which Korea shared its development experience and knowledge to support the institutional and capacity building of the Iranian ICT sector through a series of collaborative research projects, policy consultations, and technical assistance, all focused on policy issues identified by the Iranian government.

Research Interests:

His current research interests include the AI (Artificial Intelligence) large model, XR (eXtended (Augmented, Virtual & Mixed) Reality), Metaverse, HumanвҖ“computer interaction (HCI), Spatial Data Science, Ubiquitous GIS (Geographical/Geospatial Information System), Culture Technology (CT), IoT, SDI (Spatial Data Infrastructure), Machine Learning, and Cloud Computing.
